pr_title: refactor: Cleanup PR-15 â€” TS18046 â€” type unknown response bodies and mock vars (~99 errors / 15 files).
title: CronResult declares three fields as required that are absent on the early-return path (see inline comment on monthly-report-cron.test.ts lines 242-248).
body:
CronResult declares three fields as required that are absent on the early-return path (see inline comment on monthly-report-cron.test.ts lines 242-248).

monthly-report-cron.ts has an early exit when pairs.length === 0 that returns only status and queuedPairs, omitting totalPairs, queuedBatches, and failedBatches. The as CronResult cast hides this. On that code path those three fields are undefined at runtime, not number. Making the three fields optional brings the interface in line with reality.

pr_title: refactor: Cleanup PR-15 â€” TS18046 â€” type unknown response bodies and mock vars (~99 errors / 15 files).
title: M1 â€” Asserted interfaces are not derived from implementation types

pr_title: feat(mobile): SubjectBookshelfMotif + ChildCommandCard polish + palette trim + jest fix
title: GC1 lint bypass in ShelfRow.test.tsx - wrong gc1-allow placement
body:
**1. GC1 lint bypass in ShelfRow.test.tsx - wrong gc1-allow placement**

The ESLint rule (`eslint-rules/no-internal-jest-mock.mjs`) checks the source line of `jest.mock(` OR the line of the first string argument. The `gc1-allow` annotation lands inside the factory body on line 10 - neither line the rule inspects. The warning will fire and block CI lint.

Fix: move the annotation to the `jest.mock(` line:
```
jest.mock( // gc1-allow: ThemeProvider requires native env; unit test cannot render it
  '../../lib/theme',
  () => ...
);
```

pr_title: feat(mobile): SubjectBookshelfMotif + ChildCommandCard polish + palette trim + jest fix
title: @tanstack/react-query-persist-client peer dependency version mismatch
body:
**2. @tanstack/react-query-persist-client peer dependency version mismatch**

The persist client at 5.100.10 declares `peerDependency @tanstack/react-query ^5.100.10`, but the project resolves `@tanstack/react-query@5.90.21` (backed by `query-core@5.90.20`). The lockfile ends up with two different `query-core` instances: 5.90.20 inside react-query and 5.100.10 inside the persist client. `PersistQueryClientProvider` receives a `QueryClient` built against the 5.90 core while its own internals expect the 5.100 API - subscriber callbacks and cache-buster logic can fail silently or throw at runtime.

Fix: bump `@tanstack/react-query` to `^5.100.10` in `apps/mobile/package.json` so both packages resolve a single `query-core` version.

---

pr_title: feat(mobile): SubjectBookshelfMotif + ChildCommandCard polish + palette trim + jest fix
title: SubjectBookshelfMotif.tsx: spineStyles allocated on every render
body:
**3. SubjectBookshelfMotif.tsx: spineStyles allocated on every render**

The four-element `spineStyles` array is defined inside the component body and recreated on every render. It never depends on props or state - hoist it to module level.

pr_title: feat(mobile): SubjectBookshelfMotif + ChildCommandCard polish + palette trim + jest fix
title: learning-subject-tints.ts: practiceChipBg as the history soft colour is semantically wrong
body:
**4. learning-subject-tints.ts: practiceChipBg as the history soft colour is semantically wrong**

In light mode `practiceChipBg = 'rgba(255,255,255,0.86)'` - near-opaque white. As the `soft` background for a tinted subject card that produces a near-invisible surface. Consider a dedicated history background token or swap to `practiceHistoryBorder` (`#edbdc7`) which at least carries hue.

pr_title: feat(mobile): SubjectBookshelfMotif + ChildCommandCard polish + palette trim + jest fix
title: Query cache persistence stores sensitive data in unencrypted AsyncStorage
body:
**5. Query cache persistence stores sensitive data in unencrypted AsyncStorage**

`query-persister.ts` persists the full TanStack Query cache (child profiles, session transcripts, dashboard data) to `AsyncStorage`, which is unencrypted on both platforms. For an app processing educational data for minors (COPPA/GDPR-K territory), this warrants at minimum an inline comment acknowledging the threat model and why it is acceptable (data is non-credential, OS sandbox is the boundary).

pr_title: feat(mobile): SubjectBookshelfMotif + ChildCommandCard polish + palette trim + jest fix
title: packages/database/src/client.ts: connection string used as Map key holds credentials verbatim
body:
**6. packages/database/src/client.ts: connection string used as Map key holds credentials verbatim**

`neonPoolCache` is keyed by the full `DATABASE_URL` including credentials. While this is in-process (not a network leak), any heap snapshot exposes the credential as a plain string key. Consider keying by `sha256(databaseUrl)` to reduce surface area without changing semantics.

---

pr_title: feat(mobile): SubjectBookshelfMotif + ChildCommandCard polish + palette trim + jest fix
title: Looks Good
body:
**Looks Good**

- Inngest test utilities (inngest-step-runner.ts, inngest-transport-capture.ts): clean, well-typed; shifting from `toHaveBeenCalledWith` to `toContainEqual` makes assertions order-independent and far easier to read.
- query-keys.ts registry: key-identity snapshot tests provide a strong regression net; the `children` vs `child` inconsistency is correctly flagged with a deferred-normalisation note.
- CI nx affected (ci.yml): correctly guarded behind a PR-only condition; the pre-existing `fetch-depth: 0` and explicit base-branch fetch step make `NX_BASE` safe.
- Palette trim (8 to 5): breaking the TypeScript union type means stale references are caught at compile time. Test updates are correct.
- react-native-fit-image jest shim: root cause explanation is accurate; follows the same pattern as the existing nativewind shim; correctly unblocks the four chat/session test suites.
- ChildCommandCard routing split: separating header to profile detail from button to progress is the right UX model; both routes have test coverage.
- neonPoolCache: comment explains the cold-start motivation clearly; implementation is correct for Cloudflare Worker module scope.

pr_title: feat(mobile): SubjectBookshelfMotif + ChildCommandCard polish + palette trim + jest fix
title: Lines 26 and 60 of `SubjectBookshelfMotif.tsx` hardcode `rgba(255,255,255,0.72)` (container background) and `rgba(255,255,255,0.42)` (spine shine strip). CLAUDE.md requires shared mobile components to
body:
Lines 26 and 60 of `SubjectBookshelfMotif.tsx` hardcode `rgba(255,255,255,0.72)` (container background) and `rgba(255,255,255,0.42)` (spine shine strip). CLAUDE.md requires shared mobile components to use semantic tokens, not hardcoded colours. The exceptions apply only to `*Animation.tsx`, `*Celebration.tsx`, `AnimatedSplash.tsx`, `MentomateLogo.tsx` â€” not this component.

On a dark theme, `rgba(255,255,255,0.72)` renders as an opaque white box over the dark background. The container background should use a semantic surface token (e.g. `colors.cardBackground`), and the shine highlight can use `tint.soft` or an opacity variant.

pr_title: feat(mobile): SubjectBookshelfMotif + ChildCommandCard polish + palette trim + jest fix
title: Every other test file adopting `createInngestTransportCapture` in this PR calls `mockInngestTransport.clear()` in `beforeEach` (`daily-reminder-send.test.ts`, `email-digest-channel.test.ts`, `feedback
body: Every other test file adopting `createInngestTransportCapture` in this PR calls `mockInngestTransport.clear()` in `beforeEach` (`daily-reminder-send.test.ts`, `email-digest-channel.test.ts`, `feedback-delivery-failed.test.ts`). This file only calls `jest.clearAllMocks()`, which does NOT reset the transport's internal `sentEvents` array. Stale events from a prior test will appear in `sendEventCalls` assertions. Add `mockInngestTransport.clear();` to the `beforeEach` block.

pr_title: Surface ownership boundaries â€” query-key registry, facade hooks, prop-drill, ReportsList dedup, guard test
title: checkFile silently ignores namespace imports** (surface-ownership.ts:468)
body:
**1. checkFile silently ignores namespace imports** (surface-ownership.ts:468)
The module JSDoc says "namespace imports are all covered" but the detection loop only walks imp.named. A surface file doing `import * as progress from '../../hooks/use-progress'` bypasses the guard entirely. See inline comment.

pr_title: Surface ownership boundaries â€” query-key registry, facade hooks, prop-drill, ReportsList dedup, guard test
title: barrelMap / _repoRoot are built but never consulted** (surface-ownership.ts:449)
body:
**2. _barrelMap / _repoRoot are built but never consulted** (surface-ownership.ts:449)
Roughly 200 lines of barrel-resolution code are plumbed into checkFile but both parameters carry an underscore prefix and are never read. A barrel that aliases `export { useProgressInventory as useInv }` defeats the guard. Either wire the map into the violation check or remove the dead infrastructure and document the limitation. The current state creates false confidence. See inline comment.

pr_title: Surface ownership boundaries â€” query-key registry, facade hooks, prop-drill, ReportsList dedup, guard test
title: ReportsList.tsx` (new shared component) generates only two testID patterns: `weekly-report-card-ID` and `report-card-ID` (where ID is the dynamic report id). The third pattern â€” `progress-report-row-I
body: `ReportsList.tsx` (new shared component) generates only two testID patterns: `weekly-report-card-ID` and `report-card-ID` (where ID is the dynamic report id). The third pattern â€” `progress-report-row-ID` â€” existed in the deleted `progress/reports/index.tsx` inline rows. The comment in `ReportsList.tsx` at lines 95-101 explicitly says "All three patterns are kept so existing tests and e2e flows remain valid", but the third pattern is never emitted by the component (the container only gets `testID="progress-report-rows"`). Any Maestro or E2E flow targeting `progress-report-row-ID` in the progress full-list screen will now silently miss. Fix: either emit the third pattern conditionally via a prop (e.g. `testIDPrefix`) or correct the comment to say the pattern was intentionally removed.

pr_title: Surface ownership boundaries â€” query-key registry, facade hooks, prop-drill, ReportsList dedup, guard test
title: library retention query key is unregistered** (use-library-context.ts:77)
body:
**3. library retention query key is unregistered** (use-library-context.ts:77)
PR 1 (#250) established the typed queryKeys registry and 137 key-identity tests. useLibraryRetention uses an inline literal; the library domain is missing from query-keys.ts entirely. Future invalidations have no authoritative factory, and key drift will not be caught by the test suite. Add queryKeys.library.retention(profileId). See inline comment.

pr_title: Surface ownership boundaries â€” query-key registry, facade hooks, prop-drill, ReportsList dedup, guard test
title: Cross-granularity sort produces wrong order at month boundaries** (ReportsList.tsx:62)
body:
**4. Cross-granularity sort produces wrong order at month boundaries** (ReportsList.tsx:62)
reportWeek is YYYY-MM-DD; reportMonth is YYYY-MM. Lexicographic comparison means week-1 rows (e.g. 2026-05-01) sort above the May monthly card (2026-05) in descending order. Fix: normalise monthly dates to YYYY-MM-01 before comparing. One-line fix in the inline comment.

pr_title: Surface ownership boundaries â€” query-key registry, facade hooks, prop-drill, ReportsList dedup, guard test
title: The new error condition is `const combinedError = !hasAnyData && isError` where `isError` is the monthly reports error only. `weeklyError` was removed from the `useChildWeeklyReports` destructure. If
body: The new error condition is `const combinedError = !hasAnyData && isError` where `isError` is the monthly reports error only. `weeklyError` was removed from the `useChildWeeklyReports` destructure. If weekly reports fail while monthly succeed, `combinedError` is `false` and the user sees neither an error banner nor a retry for the weekly endpoint. The old code showed an explicit "Could not load weekly snapshots" block with its own retry. This is a regression against the UX resilience rules (CLAUDE.md: "Primary action retries / fixes the specific problem"). Minimal fix: `const combinedError = !hasAnyData && (isError || weeklyError)`.

pr_title: Surface ownership boundaries â€” query-key registry, facade hooks, prop-drill, ReportsList dedup, guard test
title: return useMemo(() => ..., [data?.subjects])` â€” React Query returns a new array reference for `data.subjects` on every background refetch even when data is structurally unchanged. The memo recomputes a
body: `return useMemo(() => ..., [data?.subjects])` â€” React Query returns a new array reference for `data.subjects` on every background refetch even when data is structurally unchanged. The memo recomputes and returns a new Map on each refetch, re-rendering every consumer. Change the dependency to `data` â€” `useQuery` stabilises the top-level `data` object reference via structural sharing; individual sub-properties do not carry that guarantee.

pr_title: Surface ownership boundaries â€” query-key registry, facade hooks, prop-drill, ReportsList dedup, guard test
title: Key moved from `['evaluate-eligibility', topicId, profileId]` to `['retention', 'evaluate-eligibility', topicId, profileId]`. In-memory entries under the old root segment are unreachable by any curren
body: Key moved from `['evaluate-eligibility', topicId, profileId]` to `['retention', 'evaluate-eligibility', topicId, profileId]`. In-memory entries under the old root segment are unreachable by any current invalidation. Users with warm cache on a hot reload could see stale eligibility data until restart. Worth a `queryClient.removeQueries` call at app boot (removing the old `evaluate-eligibility` root key) or a note in the migration doc.
